topic: Areas of Inquiry: Music for Dramatic Impact, Movement and Entertainment
subject: music

items.q: What does 'piano' (p) mean as a dynamic marking?
items.why: Students see 'piano' and think of the instrument. As dynamic, p = soft (from Italian). The instrument is actually a shortened 'pianoforte' (soft-loud) because it could play at varying dynamics, unlike the harpsichord.

items.q: Which chords form a plagal cadence?
items.why: Students confuse plagal (IV-I) with perfect (V-I) because both end on I. The 'amen' at the end of hymns is the classic plagal cadence - subdominant to tonic, with a softer ecclesiastical feel.
Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.

items.q: In what year was the world music label adopted by indie labels?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1987. “1972” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many areas of inquiry frame IBDP Music since 2020?
items.why: The real figure is 4 areas. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
